title: Dry-compound obligation and slick-diversity state
summary: Track prior slick compounds used in lap order and mark cars that still appear to need a different dry compound late in the race.
rationale: The run repeatedly found chronological slick-history features useful, and Formula 1 dry races normally require multiple tyre compounds. This is a rule-state mechanism distinct from tyre-wear pressure.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race sequence, maintain the set/count of slick compounds observed up to the current row, flag whether the current slick is newly appearing, count remaining slick-compound debt, and interact the debt with late-race and current-tyre can-finish features. Downweight or clear the dry obligation when wet/intermediate running is observed.
expected_effect: Small AUC gain with medium confidence; strongest expected in late dry-race rows.
risk: A set built from all rows in the sequence would leak future compound usage. Use cumulative state only through the current lap.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
